decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to enhance lateral heat spreading and reduce the thermal gradient under the chip. A conservative 40% increase is proposed, staying well within the allowed 2.0x step ratio and material bounds.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity improves lateral heat distribution, lowering the localized peak temperature on the chip.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards or below 85.0°C
More uniform temperature profile across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if conduction resistance is no longer the dominant bottleneck compared to boundary extraction.
High conductivity materials may be costlier or heavier in physical implementations, though this is a simulation study.
